title: tx_buf shared without mutex allows data corruption on concurrent TX
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c:467-513 (lichen_lora_l2_tx)

Bug: The function copies data into a static tx_buf and passes it to lora_send() without holding any mutex. If two threads call lichen_lora_l2_tx() concurrently:
1. Thread A copies 50 bytes into tx_buf
2. Thread B copies 100 bytes into tx_buf (overwriting A data)
3. Thread A calls lora_send() with len=50, but tx_buf now contains B data

The code comment claims this is safe because lora_send() is thread-safe, but the race is in the memcpy to the shared buffer, not in lora_send().

Impact: Silent data corruption - a packet with wrong contents is transmitted.

Fix: Hold a mutex around the memcpy + lora_send() pair, or use a stack buffer.
